title: edikt evaluator verdict
description: Structured output of the evaluator agent. Per ADR-018, the plan harness rejects PASS unless every criterion that names a shell command has evidence_type='test_run'. Per ADR-023, evaluator_output carries the specialist-domain envelope subagent-stop.sh reads to resolve gate severity.

id.description: Criterion identifier from the plan sidecar (e.g. 'C1-tests').

status.description: Per-criterion status. `met` with evidence_type=test_run is required for a PASS verdict to hold when the criterion names a shell command.

evidence_type.description: Classification of the evidence the evaluator based its decision on. `test_run` means a shell command was executed in this session and its output observed.

evidence.description: One-line human-readable evidence string. For test_run, include the command and a snippet of its output. For grep, the pattern matched. For file_read, the file and line range. For manual, a short rationale.

notes.description: Optional longer note expanding on edge cases, blocker reasons, or context.

grandfathered.description: True if this verdict was synthesized by upgrade migration for a phase that predated the schema. Grandfathered verdicts bypass the evidence gate on a one-time basis.

migrated_from.description: edikt version this verdict was grandfathered from (e.g. '0.4.3'). Null for native v0.5.0+ verdicts.

evaluator_output.description: Specialist-domain envelope. subagent-stop.sh reads .agent to resolve gates.<agent> threshold and .severity to decide whether to fire (ADR-023).

agent.description: Specialist domain identifier matching .edikt/config.yaml gates.<agent> (e.g. 'security', 'dba', 'sre', 'architect', 'performance', 'api'). Must match the specialist whose findings the evaluator wrapped.

severity.description: Aggregate severity across findings — the maximum of any individual finding's severity. The hook compares this against gates.<agent>'s threshold.
